section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports Easter candy consumption which may be contributing to elevated blood sugars.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The phrasing that this may be contributing to elevated blood sugars reflects clinician interpretation/synthesis rather than purely patient-reported symptom history, so it fits better as part of the diagnostic impression.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient has an allergy to penicillin and a surgical history of a previous gallbladder removal ten years ago.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Allergies and past surgical history are elements of the medical history, not exam or test findings; placing them under Findings could mislead a reader into thinking they were discovered on current exam or testing.
